trailingComments.value: * * TreeSet is a container that stores unique elements following a specific order. * * In a TreeSet, the value of an element also identifies it (the value is itself the key), * and each value must be unique. The value of the elements in a TreeSet cannot be modified * once in the container (the elements are immutable), but they can be inserted or removed * from the container. * * ## Container properties * **Associative** - Elements in associative containers are referenced by their key and * not by their absolute position in the container. * **Ordered** - The elements in the container follow a strict order at all times. * All inserted elements are given a position in this order. * **Set** - The value of an element is also the key used to identify it. * **Unique keys** - No two elements in the container can have equivalent keys. * @example * let set = new TreeSet(); * // add few values * set.add(1); * set.add(2); * // check whether key exists * let flag = set.has(1); // << true * // print all keys * for (let key of set) { * console.log(`key: ${key}`); * }

leadingComments.value:
*
   * Creates an empty, or a pre-initialized set.
   * @param {*} [iterable] - Another iterable object whose values are added into the newly created set.
   * @example
   * // Create an empty set
   * let set = new TreeSet();
   * // Create and initialize set
   * let set2 = new TreeSet([1, 2, 3]);

trailingComments.value:
*
   * String tag of this class
   * @returns {string} class name
   * @example
   * Object.prototype.toString.call(new TreeSet()); // "[object TreeSet]"

trailingComments.value:
*
   * A boolean indicator whether set contains the specified key.
   * @param {any} key - a value of any type that can be compared with a key
   * @returns {boolean} `true` if key exists in the container
   * @example
   * let set = new TreeSet([1, 2, 3]);
   * let b = set.get(3); // true
   * b = set.get(4); // false

trailingComments.value:
*
   * Sets custom comparison function if key values are not of primitive types.
   * Callback is a 3-way comparison function accepts two key values (lhs, rhs). It is expected to return
   *      +1 if the value of rhs is greater than lhs
   *      -1 if the value of rhs is less than lhs
   *       0 if values are the same

trailingComments.value:
*
   * Returns first element of the container, or undefined if container is empty
   * @returns {any} first element of the container, or undefined if container is empty
   * @example
   * let set = new TreeSet([1, 2, 3]);
   * let first = set.first(); // 1

leadingComments.value:
*
   * Returns last element of the container, or undefined if container is empty
   * @returns {any} last element of the container, or undefined if container is empty
   * @example
   * let set = new TreeSet([1, 2, 3]);
   * let last = set.last(); // 3

* * TreeSet is a container that stores unique elements following a specific order. * * In a TreeSet, the value of an element also identifies it (the value is itself the key), * and each value must be unique. The value of the elements in a TreeSet cannot be modified * once in the container (the elements are immutable), but they can be inserted or removed * from the container. * * ## Container properties * **Associative** - Elements in associative containers are referenced by their key and * not by their absolute position in the container. * **Ordered** - The elements in the container follow a strict order at all times. * All inserted elements are given a position in this order. * **Set** - The value of an element is also the key used to identify it. * **Unique keys** - No two elements in the container can have equivalent keys. * @example * let set = new TreeSet(); * // add few values * set.add(1); * set.add(2); * // check whether key exists * let flag = set.has(1); // << true * // print all keys * for (let key of set) { * console.log(`key: ${key}`); * }

comments.value: * An implementation of red-black tree
* Classes that regulate whether tree nodes hold keys only, or key-value pairs
* Node for a red-black tree

* * TreeSet is a container that stores unique elements following a specific order. * * In a TreeSet, the value of an element also identifies it (the value is itself the key), * and each value must be unique. The value of the elements in a TreeSet cannot be modified * once in the container (the elements are immutable), but they can be inserted or removed * from the container. * * ## Container properties * **Associative** - Elements in associative containers are referenced by their key and * not by their absolute position in the container. * **Ordered** - The elements in the container follow a strict order at all times. * All inserted elements are given a position in this order. * **Set** - The value of an element is also the key used to identify it. * **Unique keys** - No two elements in the container can have equivalent keys. * @example * let set = new TreeSet(); * // add few values * set.add(1); * set.add(2); * // check whether key exists * let flag = set.has(1); // << true * // print all keys * for (let key of set) { * console.log(`key: ${key}`); * }

*
   * Creates an empty, or a pre-initialized set.
   * @param {*} [iterable] - Another iterable object whose values are added into the newly created set.
   * @example
   * // Create an empty set
   * let set = new TreeSet();
   * // Create and initialize set
   * let set2 = new TreeSet([1, 2, 3]);
* Internal tree
copy contents

*
   * String tag of this class
   * @returns {string} class name
   * @example
   * Object.prototype.toString.call(new TreeSet()); // "[object TreeSet]"

*
   * A boolean indicator whether set contains the specified key.
   * @param {any} key - a value of any type that can be compared with a key
   * @returns {boolean} `true` if key exists in the container
   * @example
   * let set = new TreeSet([1, 2, 3]);
   * let b = set.get(3); // true
   * b = set.get(4); // false

*
   * Number of keys in the set.
   * @returns {number} count of elements in the container

*
   * Sets custom comparison function if key values are not of primitive types.
   * Callback is a 3-way comparison function accepts two key values (lhs, rhs). It is expected to return
   *      +1 if the value of rhs is greater than lhs
   *      -1 if the value of rhs is less than lhs
   *       0 if values are the same

*
   * Returns first element of the container, or undefined if container is empty
   * @returns {any} first element of the container, or undefined if container is empty
   * @example
   * let set = new TreeSet([1, 2, 3]);
   * let first = set.first(); // 1

*
   * Returns last element of the container, or undefined if container is empty
   * @returns {any} last element of the container, or undefined if container is empty
   * @example
   * let set = new TreeSet([1, 2, 3]);
   * let last = set.last(); // 3

* * TreeSet is a container that stores unique elements following a specific order. * * In a TreeSet, the value of an element also identifies it (the value is itself the key), * and each value must be unique. The value of the elements in a TreeSet cannot be modified * once in the container (the elements are immutable), but they can be inserted or removed * from the container. * * ## Container properties * **Associative** - Elements in associative containers are referenced by their key and * not by their absolute position in the container. * **Ordered** - The elements in the container follow a strict order at all times. * All inserted elements are given a position in this order. * **Set** - The value of an element is also the key used to identify it. * **Unique keys** - No two elements in the container can have equivalent keys. * @example * let set = new TreeSet(); * // add few values * set.add(1); * set.add(2); * // check whether key exists * let flag = set.has(1); // << true * // print all keys * for (let key of set) { * console.log(`key: ${key}`); * }

TreeSet constructor accepts only iterable objects

*
   * String tag of this class
   * @returns {string} class name
   * @example
   * Object.prototype.toString.call(new TreeSet()); // "[object TreeSet]"

*
   * A boolean indicator whether set contains the specified key.
   * @param {any} key - a value of any type that can be compared with a key
   * @returns {boolean} `true` if key exists in the container
   * @example
   * let set = new TreeSet([1, 2, 3]);
   * let b = set.get(3); // true
   * b = set.get(4); // false

*
   * Number of keys in the set.
   * @returns {number} count of elements in the container

*
   * Sets custom comparison function if key values are not of primitive types.
   * Callback is a 3-way comparison function accepts two key values (lhs, rhs). It is expected to return
   *      +1 if the value of rhs is greater than lhs
   *      -1 if the value of rhs is less than lhs
   *       0 if values are the same

*
   * Returns first element of the container, or undefined if container is empty
   * @returns {any} first element of the container, or undefined if container is empty
   * @example
   * let set = new TreeSet([1, 2, 3]);
   * let first = set.first(); // 1

*
   * Returns last element of the container, or undefined if container is empty
   * @returns {any} last element of the container, or undefined if container is empty
   * @example
   * let set = new TreeSet([1, 2, 3]);
   * let last = set.last(); // 3
